title: Mormolyca sotoana
taxonomy: Mormolyca sotoana is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cymbidieae. Within the genus Mormolyca, it is considered an endemic species native to Mexico. The classification of Mormolyca sotoana is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics. Its classification as an orchid species places it within the broader group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.

intro: Mormolyca sotoana, also known as Soto's bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Mormolyca sotoana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
